w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh wp14

w:r.w:t: v2.2 — Added Section 11: client-approved feature additions (New Item auto-category, bulk pricing tool, customer-first payments, sales reports, PDF email delivery, price list generator, customer mobile app, sales rep mobile app, on-premises hosting, full JAWS/
Complete screen-by-screen functional spec for the clone build, derived from live system screenshots
Laravel master codebase (per JAPS Tech standard cloneable POS architecture) — hosted on-premises at Continental's site (see Section 11.10), not cloud-hosted
Michigan Treasury Tobacco XML Filing — Secondary Wholesaler & Unclassified Acquirer (see separate spec)

Continental Wholesale currently runs their operation on Chief 5.0, a Windows desktop ERP/POS application built by Alpha Solutions. The objective of this build is to replicate Chief's core functionality as a module within JAPS Tech's Laravel-based POS platform, giving Continental an independently owned, on-premises system (hosted locally at their site — see Section 11.10) with the same operational workflow they already use day to day.

every screen captured from the live production system — including all sub-tabs of every form — organized by functional module with confirmed fields, behaviors, and inter-module dependencies. This version supersedes v1.0 and closes all open items that were flagged for follow-up.
Sales — New Sales Order (3 tabs), Sales Orders list, Invoices, Payments & Credits, Customers (4 tabs), New Customer
Inventory — Items list, New Item (6 tabs fully documented), Stock Counts list, New Stock Count (3 tabs)
Purchasing — Purchase Orders list, New Purchase Order (2 tabs fully documented), Inventory
, Return to Vendor, Suppliers list, New Supplier
Single active site confirmed: all screenshots show Site: WS only. Build with site support in the data model but no need for multi-site UI at launch.
Payments IS in scope: Image 21 (new batch) confirms the Payments & Credits modal is actively used.
as a modal/overlay launched from the Invoices list, not a separate screen.
Cigarette tax is a per-customer setting: confirmed on the Customer General Information tab (Price Level, Cigarette Tax, Discount Schedule, Purchase Limits Schedule) —
directly to the tobacco XML filing module.
FEIN No. appears on both the Customer Account Summary tab and the New Supplier form — critical field for Michigan Treasury tobacco tax XML filing; must be stored and surfaced accurately.
Barcode/UPC confirmed: New Item Options & Comments tab shows a Barcode format dropdown — build barcode scanning support into the item-entry fields on Sales Orders and Stock Counts.
All form tabs use a consistent 3-tab bottom navigation pattern (General / Expand or Items / Comments or Shipping Info) —
as a reusable tabbed form component.
User/role tracking: status bar always shows User, Site, Company —
as a persistent global session context.
Selects active company/tenant — e.g., 'Continental Wholesale Inc'
dashboard. Status bar (User, Site, Company) populates from session context and persists across all screens.

Four module tiles: Sales, Inventory, Purchasing, Inquiries. Each shows a sub-menu list of its screens. Persistent low-stock alert banner at bottom-left: 'X item(s) running low and should be ordered soon' — live count of items below reorder point.
Global top menu bar (File, Inquiry, Inventory, Sales, Purchasing, Windows, Reports, Help) persists across all screens.
Payments and Credit Memos are listed under Sales but appear lighter — confirmed in scope via Image 21 (
Transfers under Inventory appears grayed — confirm with client if needed; do not build until confirmed.
The Sales Order form has three tabs: General, Items, and Shipping Info.
Pulled from customer's Shipping Addresses sub-table
Name, Phone No., Address, City, State, ZIP — editable
Tied to system users, e.g., 'Yousef Imran'
Typeahead / barcode scan; 'Enter item code or click browse to add items'
Pulled from Items catalog on code entry
Unit of measure from item record
Defaults from item pricing; overridable per line
Applied per tax schedule on item/customer
Total Lines / Total Items / Total Qty Ordered / Total Items Shipped
New Sales Order > Shipping Info Tab
One-to-many: each box gets a tracking number
Custom Fields 1–5 should be stored as generic named columns or a JSON field — confirm with client whether they have specific purposes before hardcoding labels.
Route and Ship Via dropdowns feed into delivery logistics — build as configurable lookup tables, not hardcoded
Customer ID, Name, Company, Address, Telephone
Quick-filter dropdown (e.g., 'Not Invoiced') + Favorite Lists sidebar (All Orders, New Orders, Not Invoiced, This Month, Today & Yesterday).
Subtotal, Total Discount, Trade Discount, Freight, Miscellaneous
Status filter dropdown (e.g., 'NOT PAID') + Favorite Lists sidebar.
Clicking an invoice opens the Payments & Credits modal (see 4.4).
Screen: Payments & Credits Modal (opened from Invoices)
This is a modal overlay, not a standalone screen. It shows full invoice context plus two sub-tables for payments and credits, and triggers a Payment entry popup.
Order No., Order Date, Invoice No., Invoice Date
Customer name, address, telephone — pulled from order
Subtotal, Trade Discount, Freight, Miscellaneous, Total
Payment Date, Payment Method, Amount, Comments — one row per payment
Memo No., Memo Date, Order No., Amount — links credit memos
New Total, Total Credits, Total Payments, Invoice Balance
Build Invoice Balance as a live computed field: Invoice Total −
Collected Payments) − sum(Applied Credits). Never store it as a static value.
'Save & Print' button suggests a printable receipt is generated on payment — build a simple payment receipt PDF output.

Driver field on the invoice context is notable for a wholesale distributor — confirm if driver assignment happens at order or invoice stage, as this may feed into a route/delivery management feature not yet fully visible in the screenshots.
Address, City, State, Zip code, Country
Name, Address, Telephone, Fax No., Class, Primary flag — multiple ship-to locations per customer
Customer pricing tier — feeds item price lookup on sales orders
Customer-specific cigarette tax classification — feeds tobacco XML filing
Catalog Mailings, Email Marketing, Telemarketing Calls, Mobile Marketing, All
Federal EIN — required for tobacco XML filing
e.g., Net 30 — defaults to Sales Order Shipping Info tab
Manual entry — used for Available Credit calculation
Negative Points — Number of Bad Checks
Manually incremented or auto on returned check
Free-text alert shown when customer is selected on an order
Messages & Alerts should display as a warning popup or banner when this customer is selected on a New Sales Order — important for credit-hold or flagged accounts.
Available Credit should block or warn when a new order would exceed Credit Limit — confirm with client whether this is a hard block or a soft warning.
Day of week customer orders on — feeds route scheduling
Whether driver can collect returned goods
Enables Certificate No. and Exp. Date fields
Owner/Grantor — Address, City, State, Zip, Country
SSN field must be stored encrypted at rest and masked in the UI (show last 4 only unless user has elevated permission). Do not store in plain text.
Tax Exempt Certificate Exp. Date should trigger an alert when a sales order is placed for a tax-exempt customer whose certificate has expired or is within 30 days of expiry.
Primary lookup key used across all modules
10,260 items in production — implement server-side pagination, do not render all rows client-side.
Left sidebar category tree is dynamically generated from Department/Category/Subcategory hierarchy on the item record.
Level 2 — filters based on Department selection
Level 3 — filters based on Category selection
Multiple barcodes per item; one marked Primary UPC for scanning
Defines base/pricing/display UOM conversions for this item
dashboard when Available Qty falls below this
Target quantity to reorder up to
Used for suggested order date calculation
Site, In Stock, Allocated, On Order, Back Order, Available, Last Counted — read-only, computed from transactions
Opens inventory journal/transaction log for this item
dashboard low-stock alert (Section 3) — this is the threshold, not a separate setting.
Current Quantities grid is computed/aggregated, not manually editable — values update as Sales Orders, Purchase Orders, and
Weighted average cost — auto-calculated from receiving history
Tax schedule applied to this item on sales
e.g., 'Flat Amount' — how prices in the Prices table are applied
U of M, Price, Alias Code — per-UOM price overrides
as a weighted average update triggered by each Inventory Receiving, not a manually editable field.
Pricing Method + Prices table enables per-UOM pricing (e.g., different price per each vs. per box) — build the price lookup on Sales Order line entry to respect this hierarchy.
Smaller product image for list views
Image storage: confirm whether images are stored on the server filesystem (file path) or in object storage (S3/equivalent). Build file upload + path storage; do not store binary in the database.
Supplier's own SKU for this item — may differ from internal code
Cost from last PO for this supplier
Marks one supplier as the default for auto-reorder
Supplier's Item Code enables cross-referencing on PO line entry — when a PO is created for a supplier, item lookup should also match on the supplier's SKU.
Substitution quantity ratio (e.g., 2 of substitute = 1 of original)
If checked, system automatically substitutes when original is out of stock

Force Substitute logic: if an item on a Sales Order line is out of stock and a Force Substitute exists, the system should offer (or auto-apply) the substitute — confirm with client whether this should be automatic or a prompted suggestion.
Mirrors the Inactive flag on the General tab
Item is available on the website
E-commerce integration flag — confirm if a webstore is in scope
Whether this item can be ordered when out of stock
e.g., None — inventory tracking method (serial, lot, none)
Barcode type for this item (UPC-A, EAN-13, etc.)
Message printed on order/invoice line for this item
Promotion code for manufacturer rebate/deal tracking
Base unit count for manufacturer promotions
'Item is available on the website' checkbox implies a future e-commerce integration — confirm with client whether this needs to be wired to an external store or is just a flag for now.
Item Line Message prints on order/invoice lines —
as an auto-appended note when this item is added to a sales order.
5.3 New Stock Count Form — All 3 Tabs
From previous stock count for this site
Optional label for this count session
Whether multiple users can enter counts simultaneously
'Enter item code or click browse to add items' — same pattern as Sales/PO
Current system quantity at time of count
When the count line was entered
Total by Base U of M
On processing a Stock Count, the system should update
to match the Counted value and log the adjustment in the inventory journal (accessible via View Journal button on Item Inventory tab).
Variance (Counted − In Stock) should be visible either in-grid or on a post-processing report — confirm with client.
Free-text notes for this stock count session
Date PO was requested — defaults to today
Lookup by internal code or supplier SKU (from Item Suppliers tab)
Defaults from Item Suppliers last cost; overridable
Subtotal, Trade Discount, Freight, Miscellaneous, Tax, Total
PO-level totals — same structure as Sales Order
Total Items Ordered / Total Items Received
Qty Received is system-managed via Inventory
(6.3) — build as read-only on the PO form itself.
PO status should auto-update to 'Received' when Total Items Received = Total Items Ordered. Partial
keep status as 'Partially Received' if client wants that granularity — confirm.
Order Number, Requisition Date, Status, Required Date, Reference No.
Order Subtotal, Discount, Freight, Order Total
Favorite Lists sidebar: All POs, Pending POs, This Month,
Originating PO — must exist before receiving can be created
Processing a Receiving must atomically: (1) increment
for each received line, (2) update PO line Qty Received, (3) log inventory journal entries, (4) update Item
RTV Subtotal, Discount, Freight, RTV Total
and log inventory journal entries — mirror image of Inventory
Address, City, State, Zip Code, Country
Federal EIN — required for tobacco XML filing (purchaser/seller FEIN field)
Department, Contact Name, Title, Phone, Ext — multiple contacts per supplier
FEIN No. on the Supplier record maps directly to the
field in the Michigan Treasury tobacco XML schema — ensure this field is mandatory for any supplier linked to tobacco product purchases.
Departments & Contacts sub-table supports multiple named contacts per department at the same supplier —
U of M, Pricing U of M, Base U of M, Price
In Stock, Allocated, On Order, Back Ordered, Available, Last Counted
Item Code, Description, U of M, Pricing U of M, Base U of M, Price
Filter history by customer; defaults to 'All'
Ship Date, Order No., U of M, Qty Shipped, By Base U of M, Price, Ext Price, Total Discount, Total Promotions, Total
Last 7 Days, Last 2 Weeks, Last 3 Months, Year to Date, Last Year
as a query against sales line-item history filtered by item + customer + date range. Not a separate data store.
8. Michigan Treasury Tobacco XML Filing Module
Extension Module — Not Part of Chief 5.0

This module does not exist in the reference system and is a JAPS Tech addition built on top of the cloned POS. It generates compliant XML files for submission to the Michigan Department of Treasury via their e-Services portal, covering both Secondary Wholesaler and Unclassified Acquirer filer types for Cigarettes and OTP.
Filer types in scope: Secondary Wholesaler (Cigarettes + OTP) and Unclassified Acquirer (Cigarettes + OTP) — 4 return schema blocks total.

Key data dependencies on the POS clone: Supplier FEIN (from New Supplier form), Customer FEIN (from Customer Account Summary tab), invoice-level purchase/sale transactions with brand codes, wholesale prices, and schedule code classification.

Unclassified Acquirer Cigarette return includes stamp inventory tracking (R1–R6, beginning/ending affixed/unaffixed) — this data will need a dedicated stamp inventory input screen not present in Chief 5.0.
See the separate Michigan Treasury Tobacco XML Filing Developer Spec for full field-by-field schema documentation.
All major open items from v1.0 have been resolved by the new screenshots. The following minor items remain:
'Item is available on the website' checkbox on the New Item Options tab — confirm whether a webstore integration is in scope now or if this is just a flag to store.
'Shared Count' checkbox on New Stock Count — confirm how concurrent counting is expected to work (can two users count different aisles simultaneously on the same count record?).
Force Substitute behavior — confirm whether substitution on out-of-stock should be automatic or a prompted suggestion at order entry.
Partial PO receiving status — confirm whether 'Partially Received' is a needed status or if only 'New' and 'Received' are required.
Driver field on invoices/payments — confirm if driver assignment and route/delivery management is in scope for this build.
Credit Memos — confirmed as a menu item under Sales; no screenshots captured. Request a screenshot of the Credit Memo form before building.
Transfers under Inventory — grayed out in the reference system; confirm if needed.
Stamp inventory input screen for Unclassified Acquirer tobacco XML filing — this has no equivalent in Chief 5.0 and will need to be designed from scratch. Requires a separate UX discussion.
Given the confirmed data model and module dependencies, build in this order to avoid blocking downstream screens:
Core lookup tables: Departments/Categories/Subcategories, UOM Schedules, Routes, Tax Schedules, Pricing Methods, Payment Terms, Ship Via options.
Users & Roles: user authentication, session context (user/site/company for status bar).
module: New Supplier form (with FEIN, Departments & Contacts sub-table), Suppliers list.
Items module: New Item form (all 6 tabs), Items list with category sidebar, Stock Inquiry popup.
Customers module: New Customer form (all 4 tabs including FEIN, Cigarette Tax, Credit Limit), Customers list.
Purchasing module: New PO (both tabs), PO list, Inventory
, RTV — closes the loop on inventory before Sales.
Stock Counts: New Stock Count (all 3 tabs), Stock Counts list — depends on Items being built first.
Sales module: New Sales Order (all 3 tabs), Orders list, Invoices, Payments & Credits modal.
Inquiries: Item Velocity — depends on Sales history being populated.
Tobacco XML Filing module: Secondary Wholesaler + Unclassified Acquirer returns (Cigarettes + OTP), plus stamp inventory input screen for UA Cigarette filing.
Reports module: Sales report by date range and customer (Section 11.4) — depends on Sales Order/Invoice data existing.
PDF/email delivery infrastructure: invoice & credit memo email (11.5) and price list generator + email (11.6) — build the PDF/email pipeline once, reuse for both.
Bulk Pricing Update module (11.2) — depends on Items/Pricing (Section 5.2, Tab 3) being built.
New Item auto-category (11.1) — depends on the Items module (Section 5.2).
customer-first entry (11.3) — extends the existing Payments & Credits modal (4.4).
checks as each screen is built, plus one final full-application audit.
Customer mobile app (11.7) — separate phase after the core POS clone is stable; requires its own scoping discussion.
Sales Rep mobile app (11.9) — same separate phase as the customer app; depends on the Sales Order module and on the remote-access layer for the on-premises server (11.10).

On-premises hosting setup (11.10) — server provisioning, backups, and remote-access approach. Listed last here for numbering continuity only: in practice this should be finalized before Step 1, since it's the foundation the rest of the build runs on.

Continental Wholesale reviewed the v2.0 brief and approved it in full, plus the ten additional items below (Sections 11.1–11.10, added across the v2.1 and v2.2 revisions). These are new build requirements on top of Sections 1–10 above — nothing already specified changes, this section only adds to it.
Fires automatically the first time a New Item record is saved (Item Code created) — no manual action required.
Item also appears in a system-generated “New Item” list/category, in addition to its real Department/Category/Sub Category (Section 5.2, Tab 1). Does not replace the real category.
Item auto-expires from the “New Item” list 30 days after its creation date; visible only during that window.
Items list + Sales Order item lookup
Sales reps need a “New Item” filter/tab so they can quickly browse recently received items when talking to customers.
Existing catalog items are not backfilled into this list — only items created after this feature ships.
as a computed flag (Created Date + 30 days ≥ today), not a manually maintained category, so
Confirm with client whether the 30-day window should be a configurable setting or can be hardcoded.
11.2 Bulk Pricing Update Module (Cost & Sales Pricing Tool)
Brand, Item Type, and/or Department/Category/Sub Category. Must support combining filters (e.g., Brand = X AND Type = Carton) — matches the cigarette-pricing scenario discussed with the client.
Lists every item matched by the filter with its current List Price and Standard/Current Cost before anything is changed.
Flat amount increase/decrease, or percentage increase/decrease.
Apply to List Price, Standard Cost, and/or Current Cost independently.
The +/− amount or percentage to apply to all matched items.
Commits the change to every matched item in one batch.
Who ran the update, when, the filter used, and items/values affected — for accountability on bulk changes.
Require a confirmation step that shows the count of affected items and a sample of before/after prices before committing — bulk price changes are high-risk and hard to reverse.
Log every bulk run to a price-change audit table (user, timestamp, filter criteria, before/after values per item).
This is a new module, not part of the original Chief 5.0 clone scope — surface it as its own screen,
under Inventory or a new “Pricing Tools” menu.
Feature: Select Customer to View & Pay Unpaid Invoices
all of that customer's open/unpaid invoices (Invoice Balance > 0).
Invoice No., Invoice Date, Order No., Invoice Total, Balance Due — user checks which invoices to pay.
Sums the Balance Due of all checked invoices; editable if the customer is paying a partial amount.
Cash, Credit Card, Check — same options as the existing Payment entry popup (Section 4.4).
Distributes the payment across the checked invoices.
This adds a second entry point to the existing Payments & Credits modal (Section 4.4), which today is invoice-first (opened from a single invoice on the Invoices list). Build:
screen → select Customer → see all open invoices → check the ones to pay → amount auto-totals.
Reuse the Invoice Balance calculation and Collected Payments logic already specified in Section 4.4 — do not duplicate that logic in a separate code path.

Confirm with client how a payment should be allocated across selected invoices when the entered amount is less than the sum of their balances (oldest-first, proportional, or manual per-invoice override).
11.4 Sales Report by Date Range & Customer
Optional filter to a single customer.
Order No., Invoice No., Customer, Date, Subtotal, Discounts, Freight, Tax, Total, Payment Status.
Total sales, total discounts, total tax for the selected range/customer.
Confirm with client which export format(s) are needed.
Build under a Reports module/menu (ties to the existing Reports item on the
dashboard's global top menu bar, Section 3).
Same report should work both ways: all customers in a date range, or one selected customer in a date range — just a different filter state, not two separate reports.
11.5 Email Invoices & Credit Memos as PDF
Feature: Email Invoice / Credit Memo as PDF
“Email PDF” action available from the Invoices list and the Credit Memo screen.
Defaults to the customer's Email Address (Section 4.5, Tab 1) — editable per send.
Same layout/data as the printable invoice/credit memo already in scope.
Record of when and to whom each document was emailed, for support/audit purposes.
Reuse whatever PDF generation is built for the Payments “Save & Print” receipt (Section 4.4) as the underlying PDF engine, rather than building a second one.
Confirm with client whether outgoing email should route through their existing email provider (SMTP relay) or a transactional email service (e.g., SES, SendGrid).
Credit Memo form fields are still pending a client screenshot (Open Item #6, Section 9) — PDF/email delivery for credit memos can be finalized once that form is defined.
Dedicated button (Items list or Reports) that compiles a price list PDF.
All items, or filtered by Department/Category/Brand — confirm with client which scope(s) are needed.
Reflects the selected customer's Price Level (Section 4.5, Tab 2) when generated for a specific customer, or List Price when generic.
Item Code, Description, U of M, Price, at minimum.
Sends the generated PDF directly to a customer's Email Address, via the same delivery mechanism as Section 11.5.
Confirm with client: a single generic price list, or per-customer price lists reflecting each customer's Price Level/discounts — or both.
11.7 Customer Mobile App — Catalog Search & Ordering
Confirm with client whether both platforms are required at launch or one first.
Each customer logs in and sees only their own pricing (Price Level), account balance, and order history.
By item code, description, Department/Category/Sub Category, and/or brand.
Creates a Sales Order (Section 4.1) in “New” status for staff review/fulfillment, same as an order taken by a rep.
Past orders and status, pulled from Sales Orders.

This is a substantial, separate build — a customer-facing mobile app, not a POS screen. Recommend scoping it as its own phase/project with its own timeline: the POS clone's data (Items, Pricing, Customers, Sales Orders) becomes the backend API this app calls.

Needs a dedicated scoping/discovery conversation with the client before estimating: platform(s), whether orders need staff approval before fulfillment, offline behavior, push notifications for order status, etc.
Confirm target conformance level with client.
Both must be tested explicitly against real screens, not assumed from semantic HTML alone.
Login, Home, all Sales/Inventory/Purchasing/Inquiries screens and modals, plus every new module in Section 11.

Proper labels/ARIA on all form fields, dropdowns, lookups, and modals; logical tab order; visible focus states; full keyboard operability (no mouse-only interactions); accessible error messages; data tables with proper headers.
Invoices, credit memos, and price lists (Sections 11.5–11.6) must be tagged, accessible PDFs, not flat images.

Build this in from the start rather than retrofitting — the reusable tabbed form component and grid/table components (Section 1.2) are the highest-leverage places to get accessibility right once, since they're reused everywhere.
Recommend an accessibility pass (automated check + manual JAWS/
testing) before each module is marked “done,” plus a final full-application audit before launch.
The mobile app (Section 11.7) must independently meet iOS/Android accessibility APIs (
at minimum, per the client's request).
11.9 Sales Rep Mobile App (Field Sales Tool)
Confirm with client whether both platforms are required at launch or one first — same open question as the customer app (Section 11.7).
Tied to existing system users (Sales Rep dropdown, Section 4.1). A rep sees only their own assigned customers, not the full customer list.
Rep pulls up any of their assigned customers to see account info: Balance, Credit Limit, Available Credit, Messages & Alerts (Section 4.5, Tab 3).
Same catalog data as the Items module (Section 5), including the New Item flag (Section 11.1) so reps can point customers to recently received items.
Creates a Sales Order (Section 4.1) in “New” status on behalf of the selected customer — the same record type as an order entered at a desk, not a separate order type.
Past orders for the rep's assigned customers.
Requires reachability to the on-premises server
Because the POS is hosted locally (Section 11.10), this app needs a way to reach the server while reps are on the road — must be decided alongside the hosting/remote-access plan.

This is separate from the customer-facing app in Section 11.7 — different users (internal sales reps vs. external customers) and different permissions (a rep can act on behalf of any of their assigned customers; a customer can only act as themselves).
Confirm with client whether reps need offline order entry (queue orders locally when connectivity to the on-premises server drops), or whether the app can assume live connectivity at all times.
Reuse the Sales Order creation logic from Section 4.1 rather than building a parallel order-entry code path — same as the customer app (11.7).

The POS clone runs on a server at Continental Wholesale's own site, not in the cloud. This supersedes the “cloud-accessible” language in the original Section 1 Overview, which has been updated to match.
Confirm with client: existing hardware to reuse vs. new server to provision; OS and CPU/RAM/storage sizing for the Laravel stack plus database.

The customer app (11.7) and the Sales Rep app (11.9) both need a way to reach the locally-hosted server from outside the building — options include a VPN, a reverse proxy with a controlled public endpoint, or a small relay service in front of the local server. Confirm approach with client.
On-premises hosting makes backup/disaster-recovery Continental's (or JAPS Tech's, if managed) responsibility. Confirm backup schedule and whether an offsite copy is required.
Confirm how future code updates get deployed to the local server, since there is no automatic cloud deploy pipeline.
Single active site (WS) is already confirmed elsewhere in this brief; on-premises hosting is for that one physical location.
This is a material change from the v2.0 brief, which described the target system as “cloud-accessible” (Section 1, Overview). That language has been corrected — the system is on-premises.

Recommend a short infrastructure discovery conversation with the client before Build Sequence Step 1 (Section 10) — server specs and the remote-access approach affect the foundation of the whole build, even though this section is numbered last for continuity.

Local hosting plus two mobile apps calling into it (11.7 customer app, 11.9 sales rep app) means the remote-access/API layer is not optional — it is required from day one for either mobile app to function.
